req.title: Transaction dispute (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a 60-day dispute window with three distinct partitions: valid window (age < 60d), boundary (age == 60d), and invalid window (age > 60d). Additional negative paths cover insufficient funds, unauthorised transactions, and exceeded limits. Tests are designed using EP to cover valid/invalid partitions, BVA to probe the exact 60-day boundary, NEG for error pathways, DT for multi-condition rejection logic, and STC to verify the state machine transition from 'posted' to 'disputed' with provisional credit.

tests.name: test_dispute_within_60_days_creates_dispute_and_applies_provisional_credit
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists with posting_date = today - 30 days (well within 60-day window)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains provisional_credit.amount matching transaction amount
trace: R-FIN-059: disputes within 60 days shall be accepted
R-FIN-059: provisional credit applied on successful dispute
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_dispute_within_60_days_creates_dispute_and_applies_provisional_credit(
    auth_headers,        # <fixture>: returns {"Authorization": "Bearer <token>"}
    posted_transaction,  # <fixture>: creates tx posted 30 days ago, yields {id, amount}
    api_base_url,        # <fixture>: returns base URL string
):
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": posted_transaction["id"],
        "reason": "UNAUTHORISED",
        "customer_id": posted_transaction["customer_id"],
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/disputes",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body
    assert body["dispute"]["status"] == "OPEN"
    credit = body["provisional_credit"]
    assert credit["status"] == "APPLIED"
    assert credit["amount"] == posted_transaction["amount"]

tests.name: test_dispute_beyond_60_days_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists with posting_date = today - 61 days (outside 60-day window)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No dispute record created in the system
trace: R-FIN-059: disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected
R-FIN-059: error pathway returns 422 when transaction.age > 60d
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_dispute_beyond_60_days_returns_422(
    auth_headers,
    stale_transaction,   # <fixture>: creates tx posted 61 days ago, yields {id, customer_id}
    api_base_url,
):
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": stale_transaction["id"],
        "reason": "UNAUTHORISED",
        "customer_id": stale_transaction["customer_id"],
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/disputes",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DISPUTE_WINDOW_EXPIRED"
    assert "dispute_id" not in body

tests.name: test_dispute_exactly_at_60_day_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists with posting_date = today - 60 days (exactly at boundary)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created (boundary day 60 is inclusive per R-FIN-059)
trace: R-FIN-059: 'within 60 days' implies day 60 is the last valid day (inclusive)
BVA: upper boundary of valid dispute window
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_dispute_exactly_at_60_day_boundary_is_accepted(
    auth_headers,
    boundary_transaction,  # <fixture>: creates tx posted exactly 60 days ago, yields {id, amount, customer_id}
    api_base_url,
):
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": boundary_transaction["id"],
        "reason": "UNAUTHORISED",
        "customer_id": boundary_transaction["customer_id"],
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/disputes",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Day-60 boundary should be accepted (201), got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body
    assert body["provisional_credit"]["status"] == "APPLIED"

tests.name: test_dispute_on_insufficient_funds_transaction_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists with status='DECLINED_INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS' posted within 60 days
Insufficient-funds transactions are not eligible for dispute per business rules
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No dispute or provisional credit created
trace: R-FIN-059: negative path - insufficient funds transaction must be tested
DT: decision column where transaction_type=INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS => reject
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_dispute_on_insufficient_funds_transaction_returns_422(
    auth_headers,
    insufficient_funds_transaction,  # <fixture>: tx with DECLINED_INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS status, age < 60d
    api_base_url,
):
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": insufficient_funds_transaction["id"],
        "reason": "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS",
        "customer_id": insufficient_funds_transaction["customer_id"],
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/disputes",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "INELIGIBLE_TRANSACTION_TYPE"

tests.name: test_dispute_creates_state_transition_from_posted_to_disputed
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists with status='POSTED' and age < 60 days
No existing dispute for this transaction
actions: Record transaction status before filing dispute
trace: R-FIN-059: successful dispute triggers state change on transaction entity
STC: POSTED -> DISPUTED state transition coverage
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_dispute_creates_state_transition_from_posted_to_disputed(
    auth_headers,
    posted_transaction,  # <fixture>: tx posted 10 days ago, status=POSTED
    api_base_url,
):
    tx_id = posted_transaction["id"]
    pre = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/transactions/{tx_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["status"] == "POSTED"

    payload = {
        "transaction_id": tx_id,
        "reason": "UNAUTHORISED",
        "customer_id": posted_transaction["customer_id"],
    }
    dispute_resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/disputes", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert dispute_resp.status_code == 201, f"Dispute failed: {dispute_resp.text}"
    assert dispute_resp.json()["dispute"]["status"] == "OPEN"

    post = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/transactions/{tx_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert post.status_code == 200
    assert post.json()["status"] == "DISPUTED"

tests.name: test_dispute_exceeding_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer has already reached the maximum allowed open disputes (configurable limit)
A new valid posted transaction exists within the 60-day window
actions: POST /disputes with {transaction_id, reason='UNAUTHORISED', customer_id} when dispute limit is exceeded
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No new dispute or provisional credit created
trace: R-FIN-059: negative path - exceeded limits must be tested
NEG: system rejects dispute when customer has exceeded allowed dispute count
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_dispute_exceeding_limit_returns_422(
    auth_headers,
    dispute_limit_exceeded_customer,  # <fixture>: customer already at max open disputes; yields {customer_id, transaction_id}
    api_base_url,
):
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": dispute_limit_exceeded_customer["transaction_id"],
        "reason": "UNAUTHORISED",
        "customer_id": dispute_limit_exceeded_customer["customer_id"],
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/disputes",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DISPUTE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    assert "dispute_id" not in body
